hypothesis: If a stereochemistry-aware diffusion model (LoQI) is trained on a large-scale dataset of neural-network-potential-optimized molecular conformers (ChEMBL3D), then it will generate low-energy molecular conformers with near-quantum-mechanical accuracy, correct stereochemistry, and competitive structural similarity to experimental crystal structures compared to existing conformer generation methods, because exposure to diverse, high-quality conformational data combined with explicit stereochemical graph augmentation enables the model to learn the energy landscape and geometric constraints governing drug-like molecular conformations.
